text.title: Mobile Responsive Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules grounding the Mobile Responsive methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: All component styles default to mobile (smallest breakpoint); larger breakpoints override. Desktop-first cascade (`max-width` media queries) is rejected.
rule.rationale: Mobile-first cascade ships less mobile CSS and avoids hidden-on-desktop bugs.

rule.statement: Spec declares 3–5 breakpoints (sm / md / lg / xl, possibly 2xl). Inventing per-component breakpoints is rejected.
rule.rationale: uncapped breakpoint count = uncapped CSS surface.

rule.statement: Where component-level breakpoints matter (not viewport), container queries MUST be used; media queries inside components are rejected.
rule.rationale: Container queries are the modern primitive for component-scoped responsiveness.

rule.statement: Interactive elements MUST be ≥44×44 CSS pixels on touch. Smaller targets are rejected.
Document head MUST set `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">`. Missing meta is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without it, iOS Safari renders at 980px and zooms out.

rule.statement: Layouts touching the edges MUST use `env(safe-area-inset-*)` on iOS. Notch-overlap layouts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Modern iPhone notch + home indicator require explicit handling.

rule.statement: Hover-only interactions MUST have a click/tap equivalent or alternate disclosure. Hover-only menus are rejected.
rule.rationale: Touch devices have no hover; hover-load-bearing UI silently fails on mobile.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Mobile Responsive under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
